mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding labor income and consumer spending capacity, which increases corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of months with rising PAYEMS.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above seven percent signals substantial labor market slack that depresses wage growth and aggregate demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of months when UNRATE exceeds 7 percent.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is held below the prevailing inflation rate implied by CPI, negative real yields force portfolio rebalancing into equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over twenty trading days; this requires both variables.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 2-year window, or 20-day returns are not significantly higher when real rates are negative.

mechanism_narrative: During post-crisis recovery, an acceleration in quarterly real GDP growth signals escaping velocity from the zero lower bound, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of quarters when GDPC1 accelerates during post-crisis recovery.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in industrial production indicates weakening manufacturing activity and inventory drawdowns, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of weeks when INDPRO contracts month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly retail sales exceeding four hundred thousand million dollars confirm household consumption is above recessionary levels, reducing earnings downside risk and producing positive S&P 500 returns over twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of months when RSAFS exceeds 400000.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment exceeds seven percent yet payroll employment is rising, the labor market is healing from a demand shock; this joint signal predicts a bullish earnings inflection and positive S&P 500 returns over sixty trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are not significantly positive during periods where UNRATE exceeds 7 percent and PAYEMS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above eight hundred thousand units signal residential investment recovery, lifting construction activity and durable goods demand to drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of months when HOUST exceeds 800.
